---
title: Maybe You Should Talk to Someone
author: Lori Gottlieb
date: '2025-10-31'
categories:
- Biography & Autobiography
- Book
image: cover.jpg
subtitle: ★★★★ | A Therapist, Her Therapist, and Our Lives Revealed
rating: 4
body-class: project-detail-view
include-before-body:
  text: '<div style=''margin-top: 50px;''></div>'
---


<style>
  #quarto-margin-sidebar {
    padding-top: 50px;
  }
</style>

# Maybe You Should Talk to Someone
Took quite long to finish the book but really liked it, enjoy reading about human relationships and how a therapist would view these relationships

-“Infant (hope)—trust versus mistrust
Toddler (will)—autonomy versus shame
Preschooler (purpose)—initiative versus guilt
School-age child (competence)—industry versus inferiority
Adolescent (fidelity)—identity versus role confusion
Young adult (love)—intimacy versus isolation
Middle-aged adult (care)—generativity versus stagnation
Older adult (wisdom)—integrity versus despair”

## Reading Journey

- 2025-10-31: Finished
